description: Tax evasion via unreported foreign bank account with FBAR violation and wire transfers from Swiss private bank
parsed_text:
SUSPICIOUS ACTIVITY REPORT
SAR ID: SAR-2024-02788
Filing Institution: First National Wealth Management Bank
Filing Date: 2024-10-25
Subject: Harold James Glenbrook   Tax ID: XXX-XX-5509
Subject Address: 27 Ridgewood Estates Drive, Greenwich, CT 06830
Account: XXXX1148
Activity Dates: 2024-04-15 to 2024-09-30
Suspicious Activity Type: TaxEvasion
Amount Involved: USD 1,920,000.00   Transaction Count: 6
Law Enforcement Contacted: No
Branch: Greenwich Wealth Management Center
Investigator: Frances Holloway

Narrative: First National Wealth Management Bank is filing this Suspicious Activity Report regarding suspected tax evasion and Bank Secrecy Act FBAR non-compliance involving the personal account of Harold James Glenbrook (date of birth: 07/29/1959), a high-net-worth private banking client and retired equity partner of a consulting firm.

Between April 15 and September 30, 2024, the bank received six (6) incoming wire transfers totaling $1,920,000 into Mr. Glenbrook's personal checking account. All six wires originated from an account at Helvetica Private Bank AG in Geneva, Switzerland, in the name of 'Glenbrook Family Trust.' The wires were received in amounts of $250,000 to $420,000 and described in the payment reference field as 'personal distribution' or 'trust disbursement.'

During an annual relationship review conducted in August 2024, the bank's private banking officer inquired about the existence of foreign financial accounts in connection with the client's Know Your Customer (KYC) refresh. Mr. Glenbrook initially denied holding any foreign accounts. When presented with the incoming wire documentation, he amended his disclosure to acknowledge a trust account at Helvetica Private Bank AG but stated he was 'advised by prior counsel' that the account was not reportable.

A review of publicly available FinCEN FBAR filing records (where accessible through customer attestation) indicated no FBAR filings consistent with Mr. Glenbrook's identity for tax years 2021, 2022, or 2023. The incoming wire amounts are inconsistent with the client's domestic investment account distributions and tax return summaries previously provided to the bank's compliance team.

The bank also notes that the Glenbrook Family Trust was established in the British Virgin Islands in 2011, a jurisdiction commonly associated with tax-minimization structures. No prior SARs are on file for Mr. Glenbrook. The bank has not contacted law enforcement but is providing this report to FinCEN.

expected_json.narrative_summary: A private banking client received $1,920,000 in wire transfers from an undisclosed Swiss private bank trust account over six months, initially denying the account's existence during a KYC review, with no FBAR filings identified for the associated foreign account across three prior tax years. The pattern is consistent with tax evasion and willful FBAR non-compliance.
